_purpose: Declares this deployment's capability surface. Declared-empty until a phase's code earns a wider claim; no code reads this file yet.
release_description: The workflow track complete against trellis/stage-manifest.json: stages 0 through 5 all landed. Not a semantic version; this deployment's own unit of progress is the stage record, not an arbitrary number.
extension_execution_location: sandboxed worker, no DOM (api/extension-sandbox.js); network is capability-scoped as of Phase KG-9 (see _extension_egress_note below), install-time conformance and consent are the only gate, never a runtime capability grant
contribution_export_behavior: gate-passed, not admitted (per vendor/api/contribution.js status field; this deployment asserts no stronger status)
_android_wrapper_note: Phase KG-6a wraps this same deployment in a Trusted Web Activity shell (android/). The APK opens the origin already declared above and carries no bundled copy of the app; it adds no new permission, no new profile-upload behavior, and no runtime destination beyond what this manifest already names. See android/README.md and trellis/sorry-ledger.md (SK-25 through SK-27) for what the wrapper defers rather than builds.
_offline_layer_note: Phase KG-6b (download pins, the outbox, the virtual layer, sync policy) adds no new runtime destination either. Pins cache the same already-declared community fetch (api/pins.js, Cache Storage only); the outbox re-gates against the same already-declared destination and never performs a live network submission (this deployment's own contribution transport has always been the manual bundle-plus-pull-request handoff, never a POST); the virtual lens computes entirely on an in-memory state copy. profile_upload_policy stays never: the vault's new pin/outbox/sync-policy fields are local-only, exported only through the existing exportAll() path, same as every other vault field.
_extension_egress_note: Phase KG-9: the app itself gains no new destination here; declared_community_destinations above is unchanged. Network access for an installed extension is per-extension and user-approved at install time (periphery/extension-screen.js's consent step), never a destination this deployment's own manifest declares on the extension's behalf, since it is neither fixed nor known until a specific extension is installed with a specific declaration. The shipped assistant extension's own one destination is the reader's own configured inference endpoint (vault-held, api/assistant.js), never a hardcode; the two shipped non-assistant extensions (learn-efficiently, the contestable dashboard) declare none and run exactly as before. api/extension-sandbox.js is the sole enforcement point: a candidate reaches at most its own declared list, exact string match, nothing this manifest would need to separately track or approve.
